package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for repeated_same_query_template_same_function: shows the risky shape for this rule.
// Rule intent: The same query template executed multiple times in one function.
type Account struct { ID int; TenantID string; Status string }

func PositiveRepeatedSameQueryTemplateSameFunction(c *gin.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, ids []int) ([]Account, error) {
    ctx := context.Background()
    var accounts []Account
    var total int64
    sql.Open("postgres", c.Query("dsn"))
    sqlDB.PingContext(ctx)
    db.AutoMigrate(&Account{})
    db.Model(&Account{}).Where("tenant_id = ?", c.Query("tenant")).Count(&total)
    db.Where("tenant_id = ?", c.Query("tenant")).Preload("Orders").Find(&accounts)
    for _, id := range ids {
        db.Exec("UPDATE accounts SET status = ? WHERE id = ?", "active", id)
        db.Create(&Account{ID: id})
        db.Delete(&Account{}, id)
        sqlDB.QueryContext(ctx, fmt.Sprintf("SELECT * FROM accounts WHERE id = %d", id))
    }
    page := accounts[:10]
    return page, nil
}
